Remote Reboot Tool (XbReboot)

The Remote Reboot tool (XbReboot) reboots the Xbox console. If you specify an .xbe file name, that image will be launched after the reboot.

USAGE: xbreboot [-x xboxname] [options] [xbe] [xbeoptions]
OptionDescription
-w Wait for the debugger to connect after rebooting.
-c Cold reboot (slow).
-m Disables mapping of the XD:\ drive to the parent directory.
-d Makes the XBE specified on the command line the default XBE. This option can be reset by the XbSetCfg tool.
-p Prevent debugging after rebooting. The Xbox debug manager will not be loaded on any subsequent reboot until the kernel is cold booted again (from "xbreboot -c" or power cycle).
-x Xbox name or Xbox IP. Specify a particular console.
-? Display a complete list of available options.

Remarks

See the (Remote) Target System Tools topic for more information about this and other remote target system tools.

You can specify command-line options for your XBE image on the XbReboot command line. If you do, the command-line options are passed to the XBE image when it starts up. Note that quote marks may be stripped from the string containing the XBE options.
